questions.prompt: Which contact role is the legal owner of the domain?
questions.explanation: The registrant is the legal entity named on the domain lease. The other roles are operational. If you're inheriting a customer and the registrant still shows the previous IT provider, the customer does not really control the domain yet — that's your first ticket.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful to run a WHOIS / RDAP lookup before starting work on a customer's domain?
choices: To confirm the customer is the registrant and to see who runs the nameservers
To set up the customer's email
questions.explanation: WHOIS / RDAP shows the registrar, the registrant contacts, the expiry date, and the current NS delegation, all without logging in to anything. It's the cheapest sanity check that a customer actually controls the domain you're about to work on.
